note: Static emergency fallback — structured for the Emergency UX UI.

T01.title: My name isn't on the roll
steps: Stay at the polling station. Ask the Presiding Officer to check the SUPPLEMENTARY ROLL.
Request a TENDER BALLOT. This is your legal right under the Representation of the People Act.
If denied, call 1950 immediately to report wrongful exclusion.

T02.title: I lost my voter ID (EPIC)
steps: You CAN STILL VOTE. An EPIC card is not mandatory if your name is on the roll.
Show an alternative ID: Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, Passport, Driving License, or MNREGA Job Card.
The polling officer will accept any of the 12 approved alternative photo IDs.

T04.title: I'm at the wrong polling booth
steps: Ask the BLO (Booth Level Officer) outside the station for your correct Part Number.
Or SMS your EPIC number: Type 'ECI <EPIC_Number>' and send to 1950.
Go to the correct booth — you cannot vote anywhere else in India.

T01.title: My name isn't on the roll
steps: Ask the poll worker to check the supplemental list or check if you are at the wrong table.
Demand a PROVISIONAL BALLOT. Under federal law (HAVA), they MUST give you one.
Do NOT leave the polling place without casting a provisional ballot.

T02.title: I forgot/lost my ID
steps: Rules vary strictly by state. 36 states require some ID. 14 do not.
If you lack ID, ask to cast a PROVISIONAL BALLOT.
Call 1-866-OUR-VOTE immediately to confirm your state's exact ID requirement.

T04.title: I'm at the wrong polling booth
steps: Many states will invalidate your provisional ballot if cast in the wrong precinct.
Call your county election office or 1-866-OUR-VOTE to find your exact assigned precinct.
Go to the correct precinct before polls close.

T01.title: My name isn't on the roll
steps: If you registered recently, you might be on an update list. Ask the staff to check all lists.
If there has been an administrative error, call your local Electoral Registration Office immediately.
If you are not registered, you cannot vote. You cannot register on election day in the UK.

T02.title: I lost my photo ID
steps: You MUST have an accepted photo ID to vote in person in England, Scotland (UK elections), and Wales (UK elections).
Expired IDs are accepted as long as the photo still looks like you.
If you lost your ID on polling day, you can apply for an emergency proxy vote until 5pm.

T04.title: I'm at the wrong polling booth
steps: You can only vote at your assigned polling station.
Check your polling card or enter your postcode at wheredoivote.co.uk.
Travel to the correct polling station before 10pm.

T01.title: My name isn't on the roll
steps: You can register AT THE POLLING STATION on Election Day.
Bring accepted ID proving your identity and current address (like a driver's license).
A poll worker will register you on the spot and allow you to vote.

T02.title: I forgot/lost my ID
steps: You can still vote through VOUCHING.
Another voter registered in the same polling division can vouch for you.
The voucher must have valid ID and can only vouch for one person.

T04.title: I'm at the wrong polling booth
steps: You must vote at your assigned polling station for your riding.
Use the Voter Information Service at elections.ca to find your exact location.
Go to the correct station. You can register there if needed.

T01.title: My name isn't on the roll
steps: Ask to cast a DECLARATION VOTE.
You will fill in your details on a declaration envelope, put your ballot inside, and seal it.
The AEC will check your eligibility after election day before counting it.

T02.title: I lost my ID
steps: You do NOT need to show photo ID to vote in Australian federal elections.
You only need to state your full name and address, and confirm you haven't voted yet.
The polling official will mark your name off the roll.

T04.title: I'm at the wrong polling booth
steps: You can vote at ANY polling place within your home state or territory.
If you are voting outside your home division (but in the same state), you cast an ABSENT VOTE.
Just tell the official you are from another division and they will issue the correct ballot.
